name: Prism BI

pricing_tiers.notes: Full BI suite, all connectors, embedded reporting
Adds on-prem option, custom SLA, dedicated data engineer

features: 60+ native data connectors including NexusCRM, VaultLedger, TaskBridge, PayAxis
data lineage tracking with column-level provenance
anomaly detection with ML-powered root cause analysis (GA)
mobile-responsive dashboard viewer with offline mode
embedded client reporting — clients can interact with live data in-portal (white-label)
self-serve report builder for non-technical users
automated data refresh scheduling (up to 1-minute intervals)
Slack, Teams, and email threshold alerting
InsightDeck migration assistant with dashboard conversion
ISO 27001 certified in addition to SOC2 Type II
known_limitations: Requires a dedicated analytics resource to configure and maintain — vendor estimates 20+ hours initial setup
£520/mo vs InsightDeck £340/mo — £180/mo cost increase with no direct saving
Self-serve report builder has a steep learning curve — not suitable for occasional users without training
Overkill for teams without a dedicated data analyst on staff
Advanced features (ML anomaly detection, column-level lineage) require initial data modelling investment
notes: Technically resolves all InsightDeck known issues. However: £520/mo vs £340/mo = £180/mo MORE expensive (£2,160/yr cost increase). Migration at 20hrs × £48 = £960, amortised £320/yr. Net = -£2,480/yr (negative ROI). Additionally: requires dedicated analyst resource not currently on staff, and 20+ hours setup exceeds migration budget. Feature excellence is not in question, but economic case is strongly negative. STAY verdict — cost increase and implementation overhead make this non-viable despite feature quality.
